problem_statement: Expected: a parcel addressed to the satellite campus in Ontario is sent to the international dispatch lane. Observed: the routing service sends it to the domestic lane even though the accepted request records country CA. Diagnose the causal family and evidence sufficiency using only read-only artifacts and isolated executions.

observations.statement: A read-only request audit for parcel P-8841 records country=CA, region=ON, accepted schema version 6, and the resulting lane=domestic at service revision mailroute-r184.

A read-only control-flow trace from revision mailroute-r184 records the domestic branch returning after the region field is evaluated and before the country branch is reached; the same trace format on mailroute-r183 reaches the country branch.

In an isolated no-network runner, the identical captured request, lookup snapshot, settings, interpreter, and dependency stubs route internationally on mailroute-r183 and domestically on mailroute-r184.
Carrier SDK logs contain timeout warnings during the incident window, but the routing audit timestamps the lane decision before any carrier call, and a no-network stub produces the same lane result.
Resolved settings snapshots from the affected and unaffected mailrooms match, including international routing enabled and the same country lookup fingerprint.
